source.title: The role of orexin in motivated behaviours

confidence_rationale: Single-author Nature Reviews piece by the discoverer of orexin, characterising orexin neurons as intrinsically depolarised and recruited under high-effort / high-demand conditions, with downstream gain on PAG / locus coeruleus / VTA in the 1.3x-3x firing-rate band. Directly supports MECH-280's claim that orexin is the broadcast-override scalar driving recruitment under sustained metabolic demand and threat, and gives the alpha_override scalar an order-of-magnitude anchor.
failure_signatures: If orexin neurons fired uniformly across behavioural states, MECH-280's recruitment_threshold would have no biological referent; the review documents a ~5x active/quiet ratio which provides the threshold's biological grounding.

The 1.3x-3x downstream excitability gain is the order-of-magnitude anchor for alpha_override. If MECH-280's validation factorial sets alpha_override outside this band (e.g. 5x), the claim is going beyond what the biology supports.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Orexin neurons are intrinsically depolarised, recruited under high-effort and high-demand contingencies, and exert a gain (1.3x-3x firing-rate increases for saturating doses) on downstream targets including PAG, LC, VTA, and dorsal raphe.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-280's override_signal corresponds to orexin recruitment under sustained metabolic demand. The multiplicative scalar alpha_override (currently 0.5, giving 1.5x maximum theta_freeze elevation) sits at the lower end of the biologically reported gain band; the literature would equally support alpha_override up to 1.0 (2x maximum elevation).
mapping.mapping_caveat: The downstream gain is reported across multiple targets (PAG, LC, VTA, dorsal raphe); MECH-280 applies it specifically to the PAG freeze threshold. The implicit assumption is that the same gain applies at PAG as at LC/VTA, which the review supports as an order-of-magnitude claim but not as a precision constraint.
mapping.source_context: Single-author review covering rodent physiology, optogenetics, pharmacology, and human narcolepsy data; integrates findings from approximately 200 primary sources.

confidence_components.notes: Nature Reviews, single authoritative author. Mapping fidelity discounted because the gain band is a cross-target average rather than a PAG-specific anchor. Transfer risk standard.
